U.S. Employment Trends & AI Impact

Two decades of structural change in the American labor market, now updated through 2025, with a lens on how generative AI is reshaping work. Employment shares, wages, and composition from the BLS Occupational Employment & Wage Statistics (OEWS).

OverviewBy IndustryBy Occupation Structural ShiftsAging PopulationAutomationGenAI ImpactWithin-IndustryTrend Chart Gallery

Overview

Total U.S. employment grew from to over 2004-2025.
Stacked occupation-group employment shares, 2004-2025. Click legend items to toggle groups. Healthcare Support and Personal Care & Service (SOC 31 & 39) are combined for consistency across the 2018 SOC reclassification.

By Industry

Employment share and median annual wage by NAICS sector. Select a sector to update the chart.
Share of total U.S. employment by sector, 2025 (sorted).

By Occupation

Employment share and median annual wage by SOC occupation group. Select a group to update the chart.

Structural Shifts

Change in each occupation group's share of total employment, 2004 → 2025 (percentage points). Healthcare Support and Personal Care (SOC 31 & 39) are combined across the 2018 SOC reclassification.

Aging Population

Population aging has driven a structural expansion of healthcare and care work — the single largest occupational shift of the period.
Healthcare occupations (SOC 29+31+39) and the Health Care & Social Assistance industry (NAICS 62) as a share of total U.S. employment.
Employment in fast-growing care occupations (thousands), 2004-2025. Home Health Aides nearly quadrupled. The 2016-17 step reflects a change in home-health coverage and the 2018 SOC reclassification, not a one-year surge.
Healthcare/care occupations by employment growth, 2004-2025 (thousands of jobs added).

Automation

Routine clerical and production work has contracted as tasks were automated and digitized.
Declining routine groups (Office & Admin Support, Production) vs the rising Computer & Math group, as a share of total employment.
Occupations with the largest net employment declines, 2004-2025 (thousands of jobs) — concentrated in automatable clerical and routine roles.

GenAI Impact

The newest force reshaping the labor market: how generative AI (since the ChatGPT release, late 2022) is moving wages, employment and inequality across occupations. Estimates come from an event-study difference-in-differences design on OEWS data with industry×occupation and industry×year fixed effects, dropping the pandemic years and using 2019 as the baseline.
Each point is the differential change of high- vs low-characteristic occupations relative to 2019 (95% CIs). 2020-2022 are dropped (shaded). “+ GenAI-exposure-decile FE” compares occupations with similar overall exposure but different task content.
Average post-2022 effect (mean of 2023-2025 coefficients, log points; 95% CIs shown) for each occupational characteristic, by task cluster: ■ Cognitive-Digital  ■ Physical-Manual  ■ Interpersonal  ■ Routineness  ■ Skills. For wages, negative = amplifies the GenAI penalty; positive = buffers.

Employment Composition Within Industries

How the occupation mix within each industry shifted over 2004-2025. Select an industry sector.
Each band is an occupation group's share of the selected industry's employment. Click legend items to toggle groups. Healthcare Support and Personal Care & Service (SOC 31 & 39) are combined for time-series consistency across the 2018 SOC reclassification.

Overviews
Source: BLS OEWS, 247 industries · 785 occupations · 774,678 industry-occupation-year cells, 2004-2025. “Median annual wage” is the employment-weighted average of cell-level medians within each group. Interactive build updated to 2025.
